package_description: Operational speed reduction (slow steaming) and voyage optimisation for domestic coastal vessels. Well-documented 15% fuel reduction from slow steaming alone (cube law of fuel consumption vs speed). Applies to conventional diesel vessels only.
rollout_limit_notes: National adoption bound reflecting vessel retirement cycles (~25-30 yr life), port infrastructure availability, and coastal trade regulatory environment.
evidence_summary: IMO MEPC slow steaming guidance; Maersk and MSC slow steaming data; ARENA domestic shipping review; cube-law fuel savings validated in literature.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2023-24 Table F (30.0 PJ domestic water transport / 30,000 million_tkm = 1000 GJ/million_tkm). Fuel split: 65% MDO (650.0 GJ), 35% HFO (350.0 GJ). NGGI check: 76.0 × 30,000 / 1e6 = 2.28 MtCO2e.
review_notes: Well-documented savings; limited by coastal trade scheduling requirements and delivery time constraints.

package_label: Hull biofouling management and air lubrication
package_description: Combination of hull biofouling management (antifouling coatings, hull cleaning) and air lubrication systems (bubble layer under hull). Reduces hull drag by approximately 10% overall. Applies to conventional diesel.
rollout_limit_notes: National adoption bound reflecting vessel retirement cycles (~25-30 yr life), port infrastructure availability, and coastal trade regulatory environment.
evidence_summary: Silverstream Technologies air lubrication performance data (8-12% fuel savings); antifouling hull coating studies; IMO MEPC efficiency measures.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2023-24 Table F (30.0 PJ domestic water transport / 30,000 million_tkm = 1000 GJ/million_tkm). Fuel split: 65% MDO (650.0 GJ), 35% HFO (350.0 GJ). NGGI check: 76.0 × 30,000 / 1e6 = 2.28 MtCO2e.
review_notes: Air lubrication particularly effective on larger vessels; hull management benefits all vessel types.
